cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out which of the two options is sarcastic. Let me break this down. First, I should understand what sarcasm is. Sarcasm is a form of verbal irony where someone says something but means the opposite. It's often used to mock or show contempt, sometimes with a hint of humor or bitterness. Looking at the two options: Option A: "Stop putting people in neat little compartments to protect your view point — good idea" Option B: "Keep putting people in neat little compartments to protect your view point — good idea" The key here is the phrase "good idea." Sarcasm often involves saying something positive when the actual sentiment is negative. So if someone is criticizing someone else for putting people into categories (neat little compartments) to protect their viewpoint, they might say "good idea" sarcastically to mean the opposite.
